seoTitle: How to Sync ClickUp with Microsoft Outlook Calendar
metaDescription: Learn how to connect ClickUp to Outlook Calendar with either a one-way feed or live two-way sync, and filter which tasks appear on your schedule.
intro: Keeping your task deadlines visible on your actual calendar helps you avoid double-booking your day. This guide covers connecting ClickUp to Microsoft Outlook Calendar, choosing between a simple one-way subscription feed or a real-time two-way sync, and filtering which Spaces, Folders, or Lists actually show up on your Outlook timeline.

steps.heading: Open the App Center
steps.body: Click your profile avatar in ClickUp and launch the App Center to browse available calendar integrations.

steps.heading: Connect Microsoft Outlook
steps.body: Scroll to the Calendar section, find Microsoft Outlook, and click Connect to begin authorization.

steps.heading: Sign into your Office 365 account
steps.body: Pass the Microsoft OAuth consent screen and select the target account you want to link.

steps.heading: Choose one-way or two-way sync
steps.body: Decide between a simple subscription link that only pushes tasks to your calendar, or a live two-way sync that keeps both systems updated.

steps.heading: Set your sync boundaries
steps.body: Select a specific ClickUp Space, Folder, or List as the source so only relevant tasks appear on your calendar.

steps.heading: Adjust what appears on your calendar
steps.body: Toggle settings to include or exclude subtasks, reminders, and closed tasks, then verify the changes in your live Outlook calendar.

faq.question: What's the difference between one-way and two-way sync?
faq.answer: One-way sync pushes ClickUp tasks to your Outlook calendar as a subscription feed, while two-way sync keeps both systems updated with real-time changes.

faq.question: Can I limit which tasks show up on my calendar?
faq.answer: Yes, you can filter the sync by a specific Space, Folder, or List so only relevant tasks appear rather than your entire workspace.

faq.question: Will completed tasks still show on my calendar?
faq.answer: You can toggle settings to include or exclude closed tasks, depending on whether you want a clean view of only what's still pending.

faq.question: Do I need admin approval to connect my Microsoft account?
faq.answer: That depends on your organization's Microsoft 365 policies, since some admins restrict which third-party apps can request calendar access.
